title: Air quality corridor planning for urban drone logistics
summary: Near-surface air quality data helps UAV operators design BVLOS delivery corridors that avoid polluted zones.
problem: Commercial drone logistics operators planning beyond-visual-line-of-sight corridors in cities must consider air quality both for regulatory acceptance and to avoid particulate-related wear on UAV systems. Static route planning ignores the strong spatial and temporal variability of urban pollution.
solution: CAMS European air quality forecasts (PM2.5, NO2, ozone) at high spatial resolution, updated four times daily, can be integrated into route-planning systems. Corridors exceeding pollution thresholds are flagged and alternatives generated automatically, supporting U-Space operational authorisations.

copernicusRole.detail: CAMS air-quality forecasts are combined with the operator's route-planning logic, airspace constraints and UAV performance data. Copernicus supplies the environmental layer within a broader planning workflow rather than a finished route.
